# UC-22.15.48 · Cross-Zone Protocol Anomaly Detection — IEC 61850 GOOSE Floods

## Description

Spots unusual GOOSE/AppID volumes between substation zones indicating mis-wiring, VLAN bleed, or malicious injection.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_sub and sourcetypes iec61850:goose. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Baseline per bay during commissioning; (2) segregate process bus; (3) integrate relay vendor validation; (4) NERC/IEC dual use where applicable. Indexes required: `index=ot_sub`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=iec61850:goose`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Substation PCAP summary feed.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `iec61850:goose` HEC sourcetype.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Baseline per bay during commissioning; (2) segregate process bus; (3) integrate relay vendor validation; (4) NERC/IEC dual use where applicable.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_sub sourcetype="iec61850:goose" earliest=-24h
| stats count by app_id, src_mac, vlan, bay
| eventstats median(count) as med by app_id
| eval ratio=round(count/med,2)
| where ratio>5 OR count>10000
| sort - ratio
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cross-Zone Protocol Anomaly Detection — IEC 61850 GOOSE Floods (IEC 62443-3-2 / Cross-zone protocol anomaly detection)** — Spots unusual GOOSE/AppID volumes between substation zones indicating mis-wiring, VLAN bleed, or malicious injection.

Documented **Data sources**: `iec61850:goose` HEC sourcetype. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Substation PCAP summary feed. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_sub; **sourcetype**: iec61850:goose. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_sub, sourcetype="iec61850:goose", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_id, src_mac, vlan, bay** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where ratio>5 OR count>10000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT historian (PI, Proficy) or the vendor HMI/operations console the operator trusts. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value (anomalous APPIDs).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_sub sourcetype="iec61850:goose" earliest=-24h
| stats count by app_id, src_mac, vlan, bay
| eventstats median(count) as med by app_id
| eval ratio=round(count/med,2)
| where ratio>5 OR count>10000
| sort - ratio
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value (anomalous APPIDs). Key fields: `app_id`, `bay`, `ratio`, `src_mac`, `vlan`.
